title: Y-diagnose: Trace fab install → installMcpClients → resolver → toml writer to find Codex MCP wiring gap
description: Re-diagnose Bug Y (rc.14 parked): user's `~/.codex/config.toml` confirmed missing `[mcp_servers.fabric]` block after `fab install`. Initial scan: resolver.ts:80-85 DOES detect `~/.codex` and instantiate `CodexTOMLConfigWriter` — so detection is likely NOT the bug. Hypothesis hierarchy to verify: (1) `installMcpClients` (config.ts:93) iterates resolved writers but skips Codex due to a filter/feature-flag; (2) `CodexTOMLConfigWriter.writeMcpEntry()` (or equivalent method) silently fails or writes to wrong file path; (3) install.ts orchestration calls config.installMcpClients with a clients-list arg that excludes Codex by default. Trace each hypothesis end-to-end. Output diagnosis to `_codex-mcp-diagnosis.md` in session folder with: hypothesis-tested, evidence, exact file:line of root cause, proposed fix scope. If diagnosis surfaces >1 day complexity (e.g. requires CodexTOMLConfigWriter rewrite or test infra), MARK AS DEFER and signal in `_metadata.outstanding_clarifications` so TASK-007 becomes a no-op placeholder.

files.change: Trace hypothesis 3 — does install.ts pass a clients filter that excludes Codex?
Trace hypothesis 1 — does the loop visit Codex writer?
Confirm Codex is detected when ~/.codex exists (initial read suggests YES)

files.target: CodexTOMLConfigWriter class — methods that write [mcp_servers.fabric] block, target file path resolution
files.change: Trace hypothesis 2 — does writer compute correct config.toml path? does it parse + merge correctly without overwriting?
Markdown report: hypothesis tested, evidence (with file:line refs), root cause, proposed fix scope, complexity estimate (≤1 day vs >1 day defer)

implementation: Read install.ts:1050-1080 to see how installMcpClients is invoked (clients arg shape, filters)
Read config.ts:93+ to inspect the writer iteration loop and any per-client gating
Read resolver.ts (already partly read — confirm Codex detection branch fires when ~/.codex exists)
Read toml.ts in full — focus on: (a) target path computation, (b) read-merge-write atomicity, (c) error handling when file is missing vs malformed
Construct a minimal repro: create a fixture `~/.codex/config.toml` (or temp dir + redirect HOME), run `fab install --dry-run` and `fab install --yes`, observe what writer fires for Codex (or doesn't)
Use --debug flag if available to see resolver output: `fab install --debug --target /tmp/fixture` and grep for 'Codex' in stderr
Identify root cause: detection-gap | writer-gap | orchestration-gap
Estimate fix complexity in hours; if >8h flag DEFER
Write `_codex-mcp-diagnosis.md` with: 4 sections (Detection Trace, Orchestration Trace, Writer Trace, Root Cause + Proposed Fix + Complexity)

reference.pattern: Iron-Law debugging — evidence-driven hypothesis elimination (per investigate skill)
reference.examples: Initial grep already confirms: install.ts:1059 calls configCommand.installMcpClients; config.ts:93 defines installMcpClients; resolver.ts:80-85 detects Codex. The bug is in the chain between these — the diagnosis must pinpoint which link breaks.

criteria: File `_codex-mcp-diagnosis.md` exists in session folder with 4 sections (Detection / Orchestration / Writer / Root Cause+Fix+Complexity)
Root cause section names ONE of: detection-gap | writer-gap | orchestration-gap with exact file:line evidence
Proposed fix section gives a 1-3 sentence patch description (what to change, in which file, expected behavior delta)
Complexity estimate: explicit hour estimate; if >8h, document set the `defer_y_fix` flag for TASK-007

Verify command: `test -f .workflow/.lite-plan/rc17-polish-2026-05-15/_codex-mcp-diagnosis.md && grep -E 'Root Cause|Proposed Fix|Complexity' .workflow/.lite-plan/rc17-polish-2026-05-15/_codex-mcp-diagnosis.md` matches all three section headers
Behavioral expectation: TASK-007's implementer can apply the fix without re-tracing the install pipeline

rationale.chosen_approach: Iron-Law evidence-driven diagnosis with 3 explicit hypotheses (detection / orchestration / writer) — eliminate each with file:line evidence, leaving exactly one as the root cause
alternatives_considered: Skip diagnosis and patch the most obvious-looking code site
Write a failing integration test first (TDD) and let it guide the fix
decision_factors: Bug Y was parked at rc.14 because root cause was unclear — explicit diagnosis is the only way to avoid re-parking
Defer flag protects rc.17 ship date if complexity surfaces unexpectedly
rationale.tradeoffs: Diagnosis adds a task hop but the artifact also doubles as future regression-context — net positive

manual_checks: Reproduce the bug locally: with ~/.codex/config.toml present, run `fab install --target /tmp/fixture --dry-run` and observe whether Codex writer is listed
If dry-run shows Codex but real install does not write, the bug is in the writer; if dry-run also skips Codex, bug is in orchestration
success_metrics: Diagnosis artifact contains evidence-backed root cause attribution
Complexity estimate enables the orchestrator to decide on TASK-007 scope vs defer

risks.description: Diagnosis surfaces a deep architectural issue (e.g. CodexTOMLConfigWriter needs partial rewrite, or install pipeline needs refactor) requiring >1 day of work
risks.mitigation: Explicit defer flag in diagnosis output — TASK-007 becomes placeholder; rc.17 ships without Bug Y fix; bug moves to a hotfix rc.17.1
risks.fallback: Document workaround for users (manual edit of ~/.codex/config.toml) in CHANGELOG until hotfix lands
